response: We propose a novel method for [TASK/DOMAIN], achieving improved performance over strong baselines. On the [DATASET] test set, our approach attains 73.4 ± 1.1% accuracy, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9% for the leading baseline, yielding an average gain of +1.2 percentage points. While this improvement does not reach conventional levels of statistical significance (two-sample t-test: t = 1.04, p = 0.31; 95% CI on the gain: [–1.1, +3.5]), our results are consistently favorable across five random seeds. These findings highlight the robustness and promise of our method, which delivers reliable gains even in challenging, high-variance settings. Our approach is easily integrated with existing pipelines and requires minimal additional computational overhead. Taken together, our results suggest that the proposed method is a solid and practical step forward for [TASK/DOMAIN], and lay the groundwork for future research to further amplify these benefits.
